description: English train-derived MFR dictionary for MultiLexNorm-style lexical normalization. Use high_confidence_pairs for automatic replacement; send review/ambiguous/context-sensitive pairs to target-token prompt/model fallback.

thresholds.high_confidence_pairs: top_count>=3, top_norm_confidence>=0.85, top_norm/raw_total>=0.55, protected tokens excluded; selected stable short forms allowed
thresholds.balanced_pairs_for_analysis: high-confidence plus count>=2, top_norm_confidence>=0.75, top_norm/raw_total>=0.25; analysis/ablation only

roflmao.top_norm: rolling on floor laughing my ass off

notes: Pure numeric tokens such as 2 and 4 are common English shorthand in this dataset, but direct replacement is not enabled by default because many numeric tokens are protected entities, dates, counts, or times.
Offensive colloquial forms may appear in the data-derived dictionary if they are stable gold mappings. Treat them only as dataset annotation artifacts, not as style recommendations.
RT/rt occurs often but is frequently preserved; send it to prompt/model unless strong context supports retweet.
